meta.name: Vehicles & Traffic
meta.department: Police Department + Public Works (Maui Traffic Code)
meta.description: govOS free public service: a plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 10 (Vehicles & Traffic) — parking rules, citations, vehicle removal, and who pays. Sourced to Municode; hands off to the County's payment options. First version, expanding.
meta.pageTitle: Title 10 — Vehicles & Traffic · govOS · Kilo Aupuni
meta.eyebrow: 12 Stones Global · Kilo Aupuni · govOS · public service

hero.lead: A plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 10 (Vehicles & Traffic) — the Maui Traffic Code: where you can and can't park, what happens with a citation or a towed vehicle, and who pays. Enforced by the Police Department; the roadway code is administered with Public Works. Sections marked expanding are being filled in; nothing here is legal advice — and never rely on a summary for a specific parking sign.

sections.title: 1 · Parking rules at a glance
sections.note: Common stopping/standing/parking rules from Ch. 10.48 (Maui Traffic Code, Art. I). Always obey posted signs — they can be stricter than these defaults.

rows.v: No parking near a fire hydrant
No parking approaching a stop sign / flashing beacon
No parking near a traffic signal
Park parallel, with traffic, wheels to curb

source.html: Maui County Code Ch. 10.48 (Stopping, Standing & Parking) §§10.48.020/.095/.110/.130. Posted signage controls.
source.expanding: full prohibited-parking list + special parking districts (Ch. 10.76)

sections.title: 2 · Got a citation or a towed vehicle?
sections.note: The general path after a parking/traffic citation or a removal under Title 10.

steps.name: Citation issued
steps.what: A police officer issues a citation for a parking/traffic violation; it states the violation and how to respond.
steps.pay: Expense: the fine · paid by: vehicle owner/operator

steps.name: Pay or contest
steps.what: Pay the citation, or contest it by the date stated. Many Hawaiʻi traffic citations are handled through the State Judiciary; County parking citations through the County's payment options.
steps.pay: Expense: fine (or hearing) · paid by: owner

steps.name: Vehicle removal / tow
steps.what: A vehicle parked as a hazard or obstruction may be removed by the police or a property owner (§10.48.210); recovering it means paying tow and storage.
steps.pay: Expense: tow + storage · paid by: owner

position.label: ⚖️ Position — James Langford (analysis, not law; a stance beside the rule)
position.text: Traffic and parking rules exist for safety and shared access, not revenue. Fines and tow fees should be proportionate and clearly posted — a citation shouldn't function as a hidden tax on residents who can least afford it.

source.html: Maui County Code §10.48.210 (Removal—Procedure); payment via the County's Electronic Payment Options.

sections.title: 3 · Enforcement & who enforces
sections.note: How Title 10 is enforced. Not legal advice.
sections.html: The Maui Traffic Code (Title 10) is enforced by the Police Department, layered over State traffic law (HRS Title 17). Note: the zoning-enforcement chapter §19.530.030 reaches MCC titles 8, 12, 14, 16, 18, 19 and 20 — it does not list Title 10, so traffic and parking enforcement runs on the Traffic Code's own citation/penalty mechanics and State law, not the zoning-enforcement engine.

source.html: Maui County Code Title 10 (Vehicles & Traffic) + State HRS Title 17. Specific penalty sections expanding.

sections.title: 4 · Who pays — the charter lens
sections.note: Labeled analysis — traffic rules read through two charters. Not adopted law; an interpretive lens beside the Code.
sections.mauiCharter: Under the Maui County Charter, the Police Department enforces the traffic code and Public Works maintains the roads; fines are set by ordinance and State law. The Charter frames traffic regulation as a public-safety function.
sections.sovereignCharter: Under the Sovereign Charter (12 Stones) lens, the public road is a commons held for everyone's free movement; rules are legitimate to keep that commons safe and open, but enforcement should serve movement and safety, not be turned into a revenue stream that falls hardest on working residents.

position.label: ⚖️ Position — analysis, not law
position.text: Keep the roads safe and moving; price citations to change behavior, not to balance a budget on the backs of the people who use the road most. Same code, read two ways.

sections.title: 5 · Rules → Code: candidates for codification / clarity
sections.intro: Provisions that, in our assessment, Title 10 should make clearer or more transparent so residents can comply and contest fairly. Citations are verbatim-checked; the recommendation is a stance beside the rule.
items: Publish a plain-language fine schedule. Parking/traffic fine amounts are scattered. → Codify a single readable fine table so a resident knows the cost before, not after.
One contest path. Citations split between County and State systems confuse residents. → Document a single front door for paying or contesting a Maui citation. expanding
Tow-and-storage caps. Recovery costs can exceed the fine. → Codify caps and notice rules for removal so a tow isn't a disproportionate penalty.

source.html: Analysis grounded in MCC Title 10; contents expanding pending verbatim ingest.

sections.text: Free now (no account): this page, the parking-rules table, and the citation navigator are all Tier 1 — free. Coming — Tier 3 (verified tools): a citation look-up helper and an AI traffic-code assistant will be verification-gated (identity-verified). Identity is verified through the payment processor — only verified-status is stored here, never the raw ID. No live billing is enabled (Stripe keys are environment references only); any citation payment happens on the County's or State's own system.
